Looking at this problem, I need to understand what makes a set "complete" - every pair of elements must have a product that's a perfect square.

Key insight: For the product of two numbers to be a perfect square, each number must have the same "square-free part" (the product of prime factors with odd exponents).

So I'll:
1. Reduce each number to its square-free part
2. Group numbers by their square-free part
3. For each group, we can take all elements (since any pair will have product as perfect square)
4. Find the group with maximum sum

```python
